title: How Much Should I Spend on a Website Redesign?
excerpt: Website redesign budgets vary enormously — from a few thousand dollars to well over six figures. The right budget depends on your current site, your goals, and what needs to change. This guide helps you set a realistic number and understand where every dollar goes.
content:
If you've recognized the need to redesign your website, the next question is: how much should this actually cost? Website redesign budgets range from $2,000 to $200,000+ — and almost everything in between can be justified, depending on what you're building and why.

Here's how to think about your redesign budget.

Redesign Cost Ranges

Type of RedesignTypical Cost
Simple refresh (template-based)$2,000 – $8,000
Small business custom redesign$8,000 – $25,000
Mid-size business website$20,000 – $60,000
Enterprise / complex platform$60,000 – $200,000+

What's Driving the Cost?

The scope of work: Are you redesigning the look but keeping the content structure? Or are you rethinking the entire information architecture, creating new content, and migrating to a new CMS? Custom vs. template: Custom design costs more but delivers a unique digital identity. Template-based redesigns can achieve good results at a lower price point, especially for smaller businesses. Functionality requirements: Adding or rebuilding features — e-commerce, booking systems, member portals, API integrations — adds significant cost. Content creation: If your copy, photography, or video needs to be refreshed as part of the redesign, budget for content creation separately. SEO migration: A poorly managed redesign can damage your existing search rankings. Professional SEO migration planning protects your organic traffic and is worth budgeting for.

The ROI Question

Don't just ask "what will this cost?" — ask "what is my current website costing me in lost leads and credibility?" A $30,000 redesign that increases conversions by 2x and doubles your lead volume will pay for itself quickly.
